conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else feels like a chore now that didn't before?
Cooking. I used to love trying new recipes, but now it just feels like too much effort. I find myself ordering takeout or just eating cereal because it’s easy.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things will get better, or does it seem kind of hopeless?
Honestly, it feels hopeless. I look at my calendar and I just see more of the same, and I don’t see a way to change it.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
It started a few months ago, after the divorce. I thought things would get easier once I settled, but it’s only gotten harder.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. My job, my social life, my free time... it all feels empty. I just go through the motions because I don’t know what else to do.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don’t see much changing. I just hope I can get through the days without falling apart.
You mentioned just going through the motions—has it been hard to focus on things like work or even a TV show?
Yeah. I can’t focus. I watch TV and my mind is already on the next thing, like the laundry or the bills. I can’t stay present anymore.
With your mind always jumping ahead, do you find yourself feeling more on edge or irritable lately?

I get angry at things that wouldn’t normally bother me. Like when the neighbor’s dog barks too much, or when the coffee shop runs out of my favorite drink. It’s just little things, but they set me off.
When you get angry at those little things, what happens next—do you feel guilty or just more drained?
I usually just sit there and cry. It’s embarrassing, but I don’t know how to stop it.
